hero.subtitle: Sworn Translator ES (LatAm) <> EN
hero.description: Connecting cultures and languages with accurate, professional translations tailored to your needs.

about.title: About me
about.description:
Hello! I am Melina, a certified EN<>ES translator who graduated from the Universidad del Salvador and is a member of the Colegio de Traductores Públicos de la Ciudad de Buenos Aires (CTPCBA).

I am a proactive, creative and detail-oriented professional, with a special focus on grammatical, syntactical and stylistic accuracy. I have solid training in legal translation and formal documentation, which allows me to work with a high degree of precision and conceptual accuracy in legal texts.

I provide legally valid, certified, and accurate translation services, ensuring that every linguistic and formal aspect of this type of document is taken care of. I work with responsibility, confidentiality and commitment to deadlines, offering clear, natural translations that are faithful to the original meaning and aligned with the standards required by law firms and companies.

howIWork.title: How do I work?
howIWork.subtitle: This process applies to all services.

1.title: You contact me
1.description: Fill out the contact form and we'll talk about your project.

2.title: I receive the files
2.description: You send me the documents by email specifying the deadline and details.

3.title: You receive the quote
3.description: I will send you the quote as soon as possible.

4.title: I get to work
4.description: I translate the message from one language to another.

5.title: I do quality control
5.description: I review the translation to make sure it's correct.

6.title: I deliver the project
6.description: I send you the translated project in the same format as the original by email.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions

q1.question: How long does it take to translate a text?
q1.answer: The translation time depends on several factors, such as the length of the text, the complexity of the content, and the type of translation. The estimated delivery date is calculated when you send me the project.

q2.question: What file format should I send for translation?
q2.answer: I accept files in multiple formats: Word (.docx, .doc), PDF, PowerPoint (.pptx), Excel (.xlsx), and plain text files (.txt). I also work with subtitle files (.srt, .vtt) and localization formats. If you have a specific format I didn't mention, consult me and I'll see if I can work with it. The important thing is that you send me the most editable file possible to ensure the best quality.

q3.question: What is a certified translation?
q3.answer:
Certified translations are legally valid translations performed by registered certified translators. They bear the signature and stamp of the professional and may require legalisation by the CTPCBA to certify the authenticity of that signature.
They are used to submit documents to public or private bodies, universities and administrative or judicial authorities, both in the country and abroad.

q4.question: What is legalisation?
q4.answer: Legalisation is the process by which the CTPCBA officially certifies that the signature and stamp appearing on a certified translation correspond to a registered certified translator and that the document was submitted in accordance with the formalities required by its regulations. This provides legal certainty to the translation before public or private bodies, but does not imply any judgement on the content of the text; it only certifies the authenticity of the signature and formal compliance.

faq.moreQuestionsText: Don't hesitate to contact me to resolve any doubts about your translation projects
